title: The Hershey Company: Deep-Dive Analysis Reveals A Tasty Opportunity
text: The Hershey Company is the largest confectionary manufacturer in the U.S. with over 100 brand names sold across 80 different countries. The company has successfully built strong brands with lasting pricing power and a benefit from a real manufacturing cost advantage. Hershey has a great financial situation, consistently outearns their WACC and has a well-managed balance sheet.

title: All Dividend Companies Have One Big Problem In Common
text: Dividend investing is struggling in the current macroeconomic environment due to high interest rates. The yield on T-Bonds disincentivizes investors from buying dividend companies, leading to a drop in their performance. Exceptions to this trend include investments with a high dividend yield on cost, undervalued companies with potential for capital gains, and companies with rapid growth in earnings and dividends.

title: 1 Magnificent S&P 500 Dividend Stock Down 31% We're Buying for My Daughter's Portfolio

title: Hershey: Demand Destruction For Chocolate And Sugar? Nahh
text: With cocoa prices nearing its 45Y high and sugar at its 10Y high, it is unsurprising that HSY has had to hike prices to preserve its profit margins. The hikes have directly contributed to its declining sales volumes during the high inflationary environment, with consumers increasingly choosing private label confectionaries. However, we maintain our optimism that HSY remains well poised for growth, especially given its aggressive M&A activities over the past few years, with salty snacks delivering double-digit growth.

title: We Cannot Get Enough Of Hershey
text: The Hershey Company has reported strong financial performance in the third quarter of 2023, including double-digit revenue and EPS growth. The firm's profitability measures have also shown impressive improvement, proving the robustness and competitive strength of the business. HSY has remained committed to paying quarterly dividends, which are safe, sustainable and well-covered by the free cash flow.

title: Hershey Should Be On Your Watchlist
text: The Hershey Company offering stability and potential for dividend growth. The company has shown consistent revenue and earnings per share growth over the past decade, with a strong track record of increasing dividends. Hershey's brand power, scale, and diversification provide opportunities for continued growth, but the current valuation is not attractive enough.

title: Hershey: Near A 2-Year Low - Why It's Finally Time To Buy
text: Consumer Staples stocks, including The Hershey Company stock, have experienced a significant decline over the past few months. HSY also nearly reached a two-year low. Investors have bailed out of HSY, likely anticipating structural headwinds as consumers could turn more health-conscious. Its previous overvaluation also didn't help matters. However, after a nearly 35% decline, HSY is no longer that expensive. Its robust "A-" profitability grade should help underpin its market leadership.

title: The Hershey Company: Top-Tier Dividend Growth With >14% Annual Return Potential
text: The Hershey Company is a powerful dividend growth stock with steadily rising distributions and a favorable risk/reward profile. Despite a recent sell-off caused by inflation fears, the company's growth guidance remains strong, offering an investment opportunity with above-average total return potential. The company has demonstrated significant pricing power, with strong net sales growth and increased adjusted earnings per share, making it an enticing option for investors.

title: 5 Relatively Secure And Cheap Dividend Stocks To Invest In - November 2023
text: This article is part of our monthly series where we highlight five large-cap, relatively safe, dividend-paying companies offering large discounts to their historical norms. title: Buy Hershey Amid Financial And Technical Strength

title: Babylon Capital® Quality & Valuation Analysis On Hershey | Savor The Dividends & Indulge In Value - Hershey (NYSE:HSY)

title: I Paid More Than I'd Like for Hershey Stock, but I'd Do It Again in a Heartbeat
text: I've been watching Hershey for years, thinking to own the stock if it only got cheap enough. Shares of the chocolate maker have lost a third of their value since hitting a peak in May.
